difficulty.explanation: Difficulty is calculated using **effort distance**: the trail distance plus elevation gain divided by 100. For road running, only distance is used. Thresholds vary by activity type.
difficulty.formula: **Effort distance** = distance (km) + elevation gain (m) ÷ 100. For example, a 15 km trail with 800 m gain = 23 km effort.

paceModel.explanation: Time estimates are based on a modified Naismith's Rule. Flat speed varies by activity type, with extra time added per 100 m of elevation gain. Actual times will vary with fitness, terrain, and conditions.
paceModel.formula: **Time** = distance ÷ flat speed + elevation gain × climb penalty. For example, a 10 km trail run with 500 m gain ≈ 86 min + 15 min = ~1h 41m.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Utanvega is committed to respecting your privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights. The short version: we collect very little and share nothing.

noAccounts.title: No User Accounts Required
noAccounts.body: The public Utanvega website does not require you to create an account or log in. We do not collect your name, email address, or any personal information to use the site.

viewTracking.title: Trail View Tracking
viewTracking.body: When you view a trail, we record the visit to show trending and popular trails. We store a one-way hash of your IP address — not the IP itself — to prevent duplicate counting. This hash cannot be reversed to identify you. View data is used solely for aggregate statistics (e.g., "most viewed trails this week").

localStorage.title: Data Stored on Your Device
localStorage.body: Your favorites, hidden trails, language preference, and dark/light mode setting are stored locally on your device using browser localStorage. This data never leaves your device and is not sent to our servers. You can clear it at any time through your browser settings.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.body: Weather forecasts are fetched from Open-Meteo via our backend — your IP is not exposed to Open-Meteo. Map tiles are served by OpenStreetMap. We do not use any analytics services (no Google Analytics, no tracking pixels), no advertising networks, and no cookies. We do not sell or share any data with third parties.

privacy.contact: Questions about privacy? Reach out to us through the About page.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer

useAtOwnRisk.title: Use at Your Own Risk
useAtOwnRisk.body: All trail information on Utanvega is provided for informational purposes only. You are solely responsible for your own safety when using any trail. Always assess conditions, your fitness level, and equipment before heading out. Utanvega is not liable for any injuries, accidents, damages, or losses arising from the use of the information on this site.

weather.title: Weather & Conditions
weather.body: Weather forecasts are sourced from Open-Meteo and may not be accurate. Conditions in Iceland can change rapidly and without warning. The trail condition indicators (Good, Fair, Poor) are automated estimates and should not be your sole source of information. Always check official weather forecasts from vedur.is before heading into the mountains.

accuracy.title: GPS Data & Accuracy
accuracy.body: Trail distances, elevation profiles, and GPS tracks are approximate and derived from GPX data. Actual trail conditions, distances, and difficulty may differ from what is shown. Trail markings and routes may change over time. Always carry proper navigation tools and know how to use them.

emergency.title: Emergency Information
emergency.body: In an emergency in Iceland, call 112. The Icelandic Association for Search and Rescue (ICE-SAR) operates rescue services nationwide. Before heading out, leave your travel plan at safetravel.is and consider downloading the 112 Iceland app.

disclaimer.safetravel: Always leave your travel plan at

about.title: About Utanvega
about.description: Utanvega is a trail discovery platform built for runners, hikers, and cyclists exploring Iceland's wild landscapes. Find trails near you, check elevation profiles, and share your favorites with friends.
about.authorDescription: Trail runner and developer based in Iceland, building tools to help people discover and enjoy the outdoors.
about.techDescription: Utanvega is built with a React frontend, a .NET 9 backend with PostGIS for spatial queries, and PostgreSQL for data storage. Maps are powered by Leaflet with OpenStreetMap tiles.
about.aiDescription: Development assisted by Claude, an AI coding agent by Anthropic, used for code generation, refactoring, and quality improvements.

v2_4.title: Smart presets & sharing
v2_4.description: Time-aware filter presets that change throughout the day — Morning 5k, After Work, Weekend Explorer, and more. Open Graph meta tags with dynamic trail preview images for rich social media link sharing. Nearby preset when geolocation is active. GPX download button on trail pages.

v2_3.title: Directions & legal
v2_3.description: Get directions to trailhead via Google Maps, disclaimer and privacy policy pages, smarter summit weather thresholds for highland trails, and improved trail condition ratings with feels-like temperature and weather code awareness.

v2_2.title: Spotlight search
v2_2.description: Press Ctrl+K to instantly search trails and locations from anywhere. Fuzzy matching with Icelandic character support, keyboard navigation, and grouped results.

v2_1.title: Trail weather
v2_1.description: Live weather forecasts on trail pages via Open-Meteo — current conditions, hourly and 5-day forecast, trail condition indicator, and elevation-aware summit weather for mountain trails. Collapsible panel with bilingual weather descriptions.

v2_0.title: Analytics & insights
v2_0.description: Trail view tracking with privacy-safe IP deduplication, trending trails discovery carousel, "Most Popular" sort option, and a full analytics dashboard in admin with daily views, peak hours, and week-over-week trends.

v1_9.title: Activity filters & admin tools
v1_9.description: Activity type filter pills on the trail list, searchable location dropdown and multi-select tag checkboxes in admin, trail stats in the edit dialog, auto trail-type detection on update, and bulk re-detect trail types.

v1_8.title: Fun & performance
v1_8.description: Shake-to-random-trail with slot machine animation, share/copy URL button, fun illustrated 404 pages for missing trails and locations, empty filter state with bored runner, and code splitting with lazy loading for faster page loads.

v1_7.title: UX & mobile
v1_7.description: Dark mode with dark map tiles, trail type icons on cards, URL shareable filters, recently viewed trails carousel, skeleton loading screens, map marker clustering, compact trail cards, and icon-only activity pills on mobile.

v1_6.title: Locations & discovery
v1_6.description: Hierarchical locations with breadcrumbs, fuzzy trail search with suggestions, location detail stats, location role display, activity type icons, and copy-link sharing.

v1_5.title: Quality & polish
v1_5.description: ESLint setup, lint cleanup, security hardening, input validation with FluentValidation, and improved loading animations.

v1_4.title: Admin improvements
v1_4.description: Split admin components, trail health dashboard, bulk GPX upload, and changelog tracking.

v1_3.title: Filters & search
v1_3.description: Advanced filters with elevation, distance, and trail type sliders. Tag-based filtering and search improvements.

v1_2.title: Maps & elevation
v1_2.description: Interactive trail maps with Leaflet, elevation profile charts, route playback, and QR code sharing.

v1_1.title: Locations & favorites
v1_1.description: Location pages, distance-based sorting, favorites and hidden trails, and dark mode support.

v1_0.title: Initial release
v1_0.description: Trail listing with search, detail pages with GPX data, bilingual support (Icelandic/English), and mobile-first design.

install.title: Install Utanvega
install.description: Add to your home screen for quick access and offline use.
install.iosHint: Tap Share, then "Add to Home Screen"
